2604.07193.title: LaScA: Language-Conditioned Scalable Modelling of Affective Dynamics
2604.07193.paper_summary: Predicting affect in unconstrained environments remains a fundamental challenge in human-centered AI. While deep neural embeddings dominate contemporary approaches, they often lack interpretability and limit expert-driven refinement. We propose a novel framework that uses Language Models (LMs) as semantic context conditioners over handcrafted affect descriptors to model changes in Valence and Arousal. Our approach begins with interpretable facial geometry and acoustic features derived from structured domain knowledge. These features are transformed into symbolic natural-language descriptions encoding their affective implications. A pretrained LM processes these descriptions to generate semantic context embeddings that act as high-level priors over affective dynamics. Unlike end-to-end black-box pipelines, our framework preserves feature transparency while leveraging the contextual abstraction capabilities of LMs. We evaluate the proposed method on the Aff-Wild2 and SEWA datasets for affect change prediction. Experimental results show consistent improvements in accuracy for both Valence and Arousal compared to handcrafted-only and deep-embedding baselines. Our findings demonstrate that semantic conditioning enables interpretable affect modelling without sacrificing predictive performance, offering a transparent and computationally efficient alternative to fully end-to-end architectures
2604.07193.paper_summary_zh: 預測無約束環境中的情感仍然是以人為中心的人工智慧中的一項基本挑戰。雖然深度神經嵌入主導當代方法，但它們往往缺乏可解釋性，並限制了專家驅動的精煉。我們提出了一個新穎的框架，使用語言模型（LMs）作為手工製作的情感描述的語義上下文調節器，以建模情感的價值和喚醒的變化。我們的方法從可解釋的面部幾何和基於結構化領域知識衍生的聲學特徵開始。這些特徵被轉換為符號自然語言描述，編碼其情感含義。一個預訓練的LM處理這些描述，以生成語義上下文嵌入，這些嵌入作為情感動態的高級先驗。與端到端的黑箱管道不同，我們的框架在利用LM的上下文抽象能力的同時，保持特徵的透明性。我們在Aff-Wild2和SEWA數據集上評估所提出的方法，以預測情感變化。實驗結果顯示，與僅使用手工製作和深度嵌入基準相比，對於價值和喚醒的準確性都有持續的改善。我們的研究結果表明，語義調節使得可解釋的情感建模成為可能，而不犧牲預測性能，提供了一種透明且計算效率高的替代方案，取代完全端到端的架構。
2604.07193.author: Kosmas Pinitas et.al.
